content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both fundamentally require editing Cache.__init__ and configure_cache() signatures in the same 2 files, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: sequential, non-overlapping regions. 1) You go first: add your  param to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), plus set_ttl helper, and send DONE. 2) I'll then pull the updated files and append my params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) AFTER your ttl param in both signatures, plus add my own helper (set_compression) and magic-header serialize/deserialize logic in separate new methods - won't touch your ttl code. 3) I'll send DONE when finished. This keeps our diffs in distinct lines/functions even though same files. OK to proceed in this order?

note: Finished compression + eviction knobs feature. cache.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ (appended after memory_max_entries), magic-header (b'DSPC' + codec byte) pickle+gzip/zlib encode/decode helpers used in get()/put(), set_compression() method, legacy headerless entries pass through unchanged. __init__.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache() (appended after enable_litellm_cache), passed through as kwargs to Cache(...), new module-level set_compression() helper, added to __all__. All additive/appended params, no reordering of existing positional args. Verified round-trip integrity for gzip/zlib, legacy fallback, and size reduction manually; existing tests/clients/test_cache.py and tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py (15 tests) still pass. Note: in my checkout I don't see your ttl edits landing in the files (still showing pre-TTL state) - we may be in isolated copies rather than a shared filesystem, so no actual clobbering risk on my end, but flagging in case you want to double check your changes persisted where expected.
